leads.name: Orange Frazer Press
leads.notes: Ohio's leading independent book publisher, family-owned, offering first printings from 250 to 30,000 copies. Specializes in regional nonfiction and custom publishing — a textbook match for Cushing-Malloy's short-to-medium run expertise. Pitch as a Midwest manufacturing partner for their custom division.
leads.pitch: Position Cushing-Malloy as a peer family-owned operation with heritage and reliability. Orange Frazer's custom division handles corporate history books, cookbooks, and regional nonfiction — all segments where Cushing-Malloy's short-run offset excellence and 1948 track record are compelling selling points. Ask about their Q2 print calendar and current printer relationships.

leads.name: Bex Kitchen (Rebecca)
leads.notes: New Jersey-based recipe creator with an active Kickstarter campaign for 'Hearty: A Recipe Book' (298 backers, over $6K pledged). Clear, funded proof of customer demand for a printed cookbook. Cushing-Malloy is a natural fit for Kickstarter fulfillment printing and post-campaign retail stock.
leads.pitch: Bex's Kickstarter proved demand exists — 298 backers pledged real money for her cookbook. Now comes fulfillment: managing print production, quantities, and shipping. Cushing-Malloy specializes in exactly this scenario. Reach out via inquiries@bexkitchen.com to offer a fulfillment print quote and explain how we can handle the production run while she focuses on marketing and delivery. Position as the partner who makes her backers proud.

leads.name: Bottom Dog Press
leads.notes: Nonprofit Ohio literary press operating five active series (Midwest, Working Lives, Harmony, Memoirs, Appalachian Writing). Confirmed email. Ongoing short-run print needs across multiple annual titles make them a consistent manufacturing prospect.
leads.pitch: Bottom Dog has consistent short-to-medium run needs across five publishing series. Email Larry Smith directly at lsmithdog@smithdocs.net to introduce Cushing-Malloy as a nonprofit-friendly printing partner with deep Midwest roots. Emphasize competitive per-unit pricing at 200–2,000 copy runs, and the e-book conversion upsell.

leads.name: Idris Doosi
leads.notes: Fitness and nutrition influencer with 1.6M TikTok followers who already has a weight loss book and an active recipe cookbook on his website. His engaged audience actively consumes his food and fitness content — a print cookbook expansion would be a direct revenue opportunity with a built-in buyer base. No phone number found via Apollo enrichment.
leads.pitch: Idris has 1.6M TikTok followers who follow him for exactly the content that fills a cookbook — low-calorie, high-protein recipes. His digital audience has proven appetite for his work, and a professionally printed Doosifit cookbook would let him create a premium physical product to sell at events, through his site, and to his loyal community. Reach out via idrisdoosi@gmail.com and position Cushing-Malloy as the partner who handles production so he can focus on his brand. Start with a sample quote at 500 and 1,000 copies.

leads.name: Fiddlehead Press
leads.notes: Founded by USA Today bestselling author Anthea Sharp with seven Kickstarter campaigns totaling nearly $70k — proven, scaled print activity. Active production of physical books for backers with a likely need for an offset upgrade from POD for hardcover special editions. No phone number found via Apollo enrichment.
leads.pitch: Fiddlehead has funded $70k+ in print runs via Kickstarter — they are print-active at real scale. Pitch as a manufacturing upgrade: Cushing-Malloy can deliver hardcover special editions and offset runs of 500–2,500 copies that outperform POD quality at lower per-unit cost. Email antheasharp@hotmail.com and ask about their next Kickstarter campaign timeline.

leads.name: Porkbelly Press
leads.notes: Cincinnati-based micro press specializing in handbound limited-edition chapbooks, zines, and anthologies with three open submission calls per year. As their volume grows, handbound production becomes a bottleneck — Cushing-Malloy's short-run digital printing is a natural supplement. No phone number found via Apollo enrichment.
leads.pitch: As Porkbelly's anthology and themed collection volume grows, handbound production becomes a bottleneck. Introduce Cushing-Malloy's short-run digital printing for editions of 100–500 copies as a way to scale production without losing the indie feel — position as a supplement to, not replacement of, their handbound work.

leads.name: M.G. Hunt (Indie Author)
leads.notes: Self-published indie author publicly documenting their complete 2025 self-publishing journey on Substack. Hosted a 'Ready, Set, Book Launch' webinar for emerging authors — strong print-launch signals and a direct-to-reader sales mindset.
leads.pitch: Authors documenting their self-publishing journey publicly are often looking for solutions they can also recommend to their audience. Pitch Cushing-Malloy as the professional printing upgrade from POD — better quality for their own launch, and a recommendation they can share with their growing Substack readership. Reach via the Substack contact or bio link. No phone number found via Apollo enrichment.
